You will own the web platform architecture that powers discovery at scale: defining how our frontend systems are structured, how data flows from server to client, and how we make those experiences fast and reliable across platforms. This is a high-scope role that spans multiple product surfaces and requires close collaboration with engineering, product, design, and machine learning teams.

About the Job

  • Set the technical direction for Instacart's home and search web experiences, including architecture decisions around rendering, data fetching, and client performance.
  • Build innovative features that help customers discover new retailers and deepen engagement with existing ones.
  • Drive improvements to Core Web Vitals, page load performance, and overall frontend health.
  • Improve SEO and organic discovery to help new customers find Instacart.
  • Partner with ML and backend teams to surface personalized, real-time search and recommendations on the web.
  • Mentor senior engineers and grow engineering capability across the team.

We're looking for someone who is comfortable with ambiguity, has a bias toward action, and can influence technical strategy across organizational boundaries.

About You

Minimum Qualifications

  • Extensive experience in a senior/staff engineer role with a strong focus on web systems design and frontend architecture.
  • Deep expertise in modern web technologies (React, TypeScript, Next.js etc.) with the ability to reason about end-to-end system architecture across client and server.
  • Proven experience designing and operating large-scale web platforms supporting multiple product surfaces.
  • Ability to dive deep into web performance optimization  rendering, Core Web Vitals, bundle size, caching strategies, and client-side data fetching.
  • Experience leading engineering design reviews and influencing technical decisions across teams.
  • Experience building web systems used and maintained by large engineering organizations (30+ engineers).
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to influence technical strategy across organizational boundaries.
  • Track record of mentoring engineers and driving long-term technical vision.
  • 10+ years of software engineering experience with a focus on frontend. 

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in Search, Recommendations, Personalization, or related discovery platforms is a huge plus.
  • Experience with hybrid architectures, webview-based client systems, or cross-platform web rendering.
  • Experience partnering closely with machine learning, product, and design teams.
  • Experience building frontend platforms or design systems that support multiple feature teams at scale.
  • Understanding of the online grocery, marketplace, or e-commerce ecosystem.
  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science or a related field.

location: Canada - Remote (ON, AB, BC, or NS Only)
description_text:
We're transforming the grocery industry

There’s no one-size fits all approach to how we do our best work. Our employees have the flexibility to choose where they do their best work—whether it’s from home, an office, or your favorite coffee shop—while staying connected and building community through regular in-person events. Learn more about our flexible approach to where we work.

Overview

We’re looking for a Strategic Finance Analyst, Product Finance to join Instacart’s Strategic Finance team supporting Instacart Ads and Enterprise SaaS business units. You’ll help shape the next phase of growth at Instacart ads by partnering on our most critical initiatives, building business cases and P&L models, and translating insights into decisions that move the business forward.

In this role, you’ll partner closely with Ads Product, Carrot Ads, and our enterprise SaaS team to connect strategy to financial outcomes, prioritize initiatives, and deliver crisp, executive-ready narratives. If you enjoy rolling up your sleeves, collaborating across functions, and using data to influence strategy in a fast-paced environment, this role offers meaningful scope and visibility.

Instacart Ads helps brands of all sizes put their products in front of millions of high-intent customers. With products, tools, insights, and automation, our ads platform drives measurable impact from discovery to purchase—making your analyses central to how we grow the pie for advertisers, retailers, and customers alike.

About the Job

  • Own critical portions of Instacart Ads revenue initiatives, including planning, forecasting, deal approvals, and resource investments.
  • Partner with Ads Product, Carrot Ads, and our enterprise SaaS team to translate strategy into measurable financial outcomes and recommend actions that drive revenue.
  • Design and implement financial processes and dashboards that leverage internal data and KPIs to monitor performance, surface insights, and automate recurring reporting.
  • Build monthly and quarterly performance materials for leadership, including forecast updates, variance analyses, and materials for board and investor reviews.
  • Conduct ad hoc financial modeling and deep dives on initiatives such as SaaS performance, pricing and packaging, cohort performance, and attribution; self-serve data with SQL when needed.

About You

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Economics, Finance, Business, or a related field; or equivalent practical experience.
  • 2+ years of experience in strategic finance at a high growth technology company, investment banking, or consulting
  • Advanced proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets, including building driver-based financial models, cohort analyses, and scenario/sensitivity modeling.
  • Experience building revenue forecasts and translating KPIs into financial outcomes for an operating business.
  • Proven ability to create executive-ready materials and present insights to cross-functional and senior audiences.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Intermediate SQL (e.g., joins, window functions) and experience working with large datasets; familiarity with Looker or Tableau.
  • Experience in digital advertising, ad tech, e-commerce media, or SaaS monetization models.
  • Experience preparing materials for board and investor audiences.
  • Ability to support West Coast business hours in a distributed, remote environment.
  • Self-starter who thrives in fast-changing environments; collaborative, low-ego approach—and an appreciation for good food puns.

title: Strategic Finance Manager, Retailer
description_text:
We're transforming the grocery industry

At Instacart, we invite the world to share love through food because we believe everyone should have access to the food they love and more time to enjoy it together. Where others see a simple need for grocery delivery, we see exciting complexity and endless opportunity to serve the varied needs of our community. We work to deliver an essential service that customers rely on to get their groceries and household goods, while also offering safe and flexible earnings opportunities to Instacart Personal Shoppers.

Instacart has become a lifeline for millions of people, and we’re building the team to help push our shopping cart forward. If you’re ready to do the best work of your life, come join our table.

There’s no one-size fits all approach to how we do our best work. Our employees have the flexibility to choose where they do their best work—whether it’s from home, an office, or your favorite coffee shop—while staying connected and building community through regular in-person events. Learn more about our flexible approach to where we work.

Overview

Instacart’s Strategic Finance team partners across the business to drive disciplined growth and long-term value creation. Within this group, the Retailer pod supports our Retail Partnerships organization as we expand and deepen relationships with grocers and retailers across North America.

In this role you will be the finance lead for retailer deal flow—from first conversation and pricing strategy through signed agreement and post-close performance management. You will develop decision frameworks, build robust models, and advise senior leaders through high-stakes negotiations that shape Instacart’s revenue and margin profile.

You will collaborate closely with Partnerships, Legal, Revenue Operations, Accounting, Product, Data Science, and Analytics. This is a fast-paced, highly cross-functional role on a lean team of 7 across pods, ideal for someone who thrives in ambiguity, rolls up their sleeves, and communicates with clarity and conviction. We work together, move quickly, and put facts and transparency on the table to make the best decisions for our customers, shoppers, retailers, brands, and partners.

 

About the Job

  • Lead financial modeling and scenario analysis for retailer partnerships, including revenue share structures, fee schedules, incentives, media and fulfillment economics; deliver clear, actionable recommendations to executives.
  • Serve as the finance partner in negotiations—shaping pricing strategy, establishing guardrails, drafting approval memos, and aligning cross-functional stakeholders from term sheet to signature.
  • Build and maintain partner- and portfolio-level forecasts and P&Ls define KPIs, instrument tracking, and drive monthly/quarterly performance reviews with Partnerships leadership.
  • Design and operationalize scalable deal frameworks, templates, and a deal desk process that improves speed, consistency, and compliance across the Retailer organization.
  • Partner with Data Science and Analytics to develop self-serve dashboards and insights that quantify tradeoffs, surface risks, and accelerate decision-making.
  • Evaluate and prioritize strategic investments and experiments; define hypotheses, measurement plans, and success criteria to inform go/no-go decisions.
  • Coach and mentor analysts within the pod to elevate modeling rigor, business judgment, and executive-ready storytelling (no direct reports).



About You

Minimum Qualifications

  • 6+ years of experience in strategic finance, corporate finance, investment banking, private equity, or management consulting with a focus on commercial deals or partnerships.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Mathematics, or a related field.
  • Advanced financial modeling skills in Excel or Google Sheets, including multi-scenario and sensitivity analyses for complex, multi-year agreements.
  • Proven track record owning the finance workstream end-to-end for complex commercial agreements—from initial scoping and pricing through signature and post-close performance tracking.
  • Hands-on experience partnering with Sales/Business Development and Legal to structure and negotiate contract terms that balance growth and unit economics.
  • Strong command of accounting fundamentals and unit economics; ability to build and reconcile partner- and portfolio-level P&Ls and cash flow impacts.
  • Ability to communicate complex analyses and recommendations clearly to senior executives in written and verbal formats.

 

Preferred Qualifications

  • MBA or relevant advanced degree.

  • Proficiency in SQL and familiarity with BI tools and building AI workflows (e.g., complex Claude prompts).
  • Experience building pricing frameworks, rate cards, and scalable deal desk processes.
  • Familiarity with revenue recognition and partnership accounting considerations for multi-element arrangements.
  • Demonstrated success managing a fast-moving pipeline with multiple concurrent deals and cross-functional stakeholders.

This role is critical in ensuring new and existing revenue arrangements and policies are assessed and are in compliance with US GAAP while driving continuous process improvements and delivering valuable insights to senior management.

 

This role reports to the Senior Manager of Technical Revenue Accounting within the Chief Accounting Officer (CAO) organization. The Technical Revenue team works closely with Finance, Legal, BD, and other business units to ensure compliance with accounting standards, SEC regulations, and internal policies. The team also plays a pivotal role in delivering accurate and strategic communications to external stakeholders.

 

About the Job

  • Research and provide detailed insights and guidance regarding the accounting treatment of various proposed contracts and product offerings in accordance with ASC 606
  • Analyze customer contracts to identify key terms and ensure appropriate accounting treatment in accordance with ASC 606
  • Draft, and maintain technical accounting memos and policies to support revenue accounting conclusions for new and existing product lines

  • Own review of quarterly SOX contract controls

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance; CPA certification required
  • 7+ years of progressive accounting experience in both public accounting and industry with a focus on revenue recognition 
  • Strong knowledge of US GAAP with a focus on ASC 606 and ASC 340
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence collaborate effectively with stakeholders across all levels
  • High attention to detail and commitment to completeness, accuracy, and integrity in financial reporting

title: Trust & Safety Manager, Critical Response
location: Canada - Remote (ON, AB, BC, or NS Only)
description_text:
We're transforming the grocery industry

At Instacart, we invite the world to share love through food because we believe everyone should have access to the food they love and more time to enjoy it together. Where others see a simple need for grocery delivery, we see exciting complexity and endless opportunity to serve the varied needs of our community. We work to deliver an essential service that customers rely on to get their groceries and household goods, while also offering safe and flexible earnings opportunities to Instacart Personal Shoppers.

Instacart has become a lifeline for millions of people, and we’re building the team to help push our shopping cart forward. If you’re ready to do the best work of your life, come join our table.

Instacart is a Flex First team

There’s no one-size fits all approach to how we do our best work. Our employees have the flexibility to choose where they do their best work—whether it’s from home, an office, or your favorite coffee shop—while staying connected and building community through regular in-person events. Learn more about our flexible approach to where we work.

Overview

Instacart’s Critical Response Team (CRT) is instrumental in managing some of the most sensitive and urgent situations on our platform. As the Critical Response Manager, you will lead a team of specialists and associates responsible for various workflows covering interpersonal misconduct, injuries, risk intelligence monitoring, and real-time public safety events that have potential impact on the platform. 

 

This is a high-visibility leadership role for someone who’s passionate about platform safety, thrives in complex and fast-moving environments, can build strong team culture under pressure, and brings both operational rigor and long-term strategic vision to their work.  You will have the opportunity to work on safety problems that genuinely matter - your team’s decisions directly protect shoppers, customers, and retail partners. You will also lead a company’s investment in AI-augmented safety operations to shape how those tools evolve.

 

About the role 

  • Define and execute the long-term roadmap for the Critical Response team, including tooling upgrades, program gap remediation and AI integrations.
  • Lead continued development of current workflows and safety programs  to improve case process accuracy and reduce operational redundancy.
  • Partner with Customer Experience, Trust & Safety leadership, Legal and Product teams to build more effective escalation and safety frameworks for the company to reduce risks. 
  • Ensure institutional knowledge is documented and scalable across all workflows.
  • Audit and streamline existing workflows to reduce administrative burden and expand capacity for strategic, proactive work.
  • Implement a clear framework for team’s operation capacity while balancing reactive case-handling and dedicated project deliveries
  • Be able to use and interpret data to surface operational insights that drive process improvements and better safety outcomes.
  • Mentoring the team. Build a high-trust, high-engagement team culture. Establish clear career paths and growth opportunities for all members in the team.
  • Ensuring all team members have clear ownership, development opportunities, and a sense of purpose.
  • Owns team performance across urgent and routine Salesforce cases and safety alert systems.
  • Ensure operational coverage; proactively manage scheduling, escalations, and coverage gaps.
  • Serves as one of the  primary escalation points for critical incidents

 

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in an ever-changing landscape, enjoys turning complexity into clarity, and is excited about driving strategic improvements at scale.

 

About You

Minimum Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience in Trust & Safety, crisis response,  or a related operations function.
  • 5+ years of background in policy development or risk analytics
  • 3+ years of directly engaging with regulatory compliance and law enforcement workflows.  
  • 3+ years of people management experience, ideally in a high-stakes operational environment.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage complex, multi-workflow operations with strict SLA requirements.
  • Experience handling sensitive situations requiring sound judgment under pressure 
  • Strong analytical skills and comfort using data to inform operational and strategic decisions
  • Excellent written and verbal communication; ability to navigate difficult conversations with empathy and clarity

 

Preferred Qualifications

  • Familiarity with SLA metric frameworks to measure process adoption and outcomes.
  • Familiarity with Salesforce case management and operations toolings.
  • Experience integrating AI tools or automation into operations workflows.
  • Understanding of SQL is a plus. 
  • Background in employee engagement and culture-building within high impact teams.
  • Experience working cross-functionally with product, engineering, legal, and communications teams.

title: Trust & Safety Specialist, Law Enforcement Response
description_text:
We're transforming the grocery industry

At Instacart, we invite the world to share love through food because we believe everyone should have access to the food they love and more time to enjoy it together. Where others see a simple need for grocery delivery, we see exciting complexity and endless opportunity to serve the varied needs of our community. We work to deliver an essential service that customers rely on to get their groceries and household goods, while also offering safe and flexible earnings opportunities to Instacart Personal Shoppers.

Instacart has become a lifeline for millions of people, and we’re building the team to help push our shopping cart forward. If you’re ready to do the best work of your life, come join our table.

Instacart is a Flex First team

There’s no one-size fits all approach to how we do our best work. Our employees have the flexibility to choose where they do their best work—whether it’s from home, an office, or your favorite coffee shop—while staying connected and building community through regular in-person events. Learn more about our flexible approach to where we work.

Why this role is on the menu

Instacart operates at the intersection of technology and everyday life — and that means real-world legal and safety obligations that can't wait. The Law Enforcement Response (LER) Specialist exists because Trust and Safety needs a dedicated, precise, and trustworthy owner for one of our most sensitive functions: responding to law enforcement inquiries, legal process, and platform safety investigations with accuracy and speed.

 

The volume and complexity of subpoenas, warrants, and emergency requests reaching Instacart requires someone who can make sound judgment calls under pressure while protecting user privacy and keeping us legally compliant. Twelve months from now, this person will have built a track record of legal response handling, established trust with our Legal team and strengthened the processes that keep Instacart's platform safe for every shopper, customer, and partner.



What you’ll cook up in your first year

  • Legally airtight response record: Every valid subpoena, warrant, and court order processed accurately and on time — with clear documentation that holds up to Legal review and protects Instacart from compliance risk.
  • Trusted law enforcement partner: Law enforcement agencies receive timely, policy-compliant responses to data requests.
  • Faster, cleaner investigations: User identification and records pulls for misconduct and safety investigations are completed with precision- reducing cycle time and improving outcomes for Trust and Safety.
  • Cross-functional credibility: Legal and other internal stakeholders rely on you as the go-to expert for LER policy questions, knowing you can communicate nuanced legal and privacy issues clearly in high-stakes situations.
  • Process resilience: Emergencies and real-time safety situations are handled calmly and methodically, with documentation that makes handoffs and future responses even smoother.



The secret ingredients we’re looking for

You’re someone who takes quiet pride in getting the details right — especially when the stakes are high. You can hold complexity without losing composure, write with precision under pressure, and handle sensitive information the way it deserves to be handled: carefully and confidentially.

 

Must-have pantry staples (Minimum Qualifications)

  • Familiarity with legal language and the US and/or Canadian criminal justice system, including how subpoenas, warrants, and court orders work in practice
  • Strong written communication skills — able to draft clear, accurate responses to legal process and explain complex policy to both law enforcement and internal stakeholders
  • Ability to stay calm, methodical, and detail-oriented in time-sensitive or emergency situations
  • Comfort handling sensitive, confidential information with a high degree of discretion
  • Working knowledge of Google Drive, Excel, or Google Sheets for collaboration and basic data analysis
  • Genuine interest in law, policy, and privacy — and a desire to build expertise at this intersection

 

Optional garnishes (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Experience with applicable privacy laws and regulations (e.g. CCPA, PIPEDA, SCA)
  • Hands-on experience using investigative tools to locate and pull records from limited information
  • Prior legal or privacy operations experience, including drafting or reviewing responses to legal process
  • Basic SQL fluency and comfort with Adobe Acrobat or similar PDF tools
